name: Qase Test Management API
what_it_does: Qase REST API for modern test case management platform. Enables AI agents to query test suites, test cases, test runs, and defect reports. Qase is a developer-friendly, modern alternative to TestRail with better API design and integrations with CI/CD tools like GitHub Actions, Jenkins, and pytest.
use_cases: Querying test coverage and test case status from QA workflow agents
Retrieving test run results and failure analysis from quality analytics agents
Managing test suites and shared test steps from QA automation agents
Integrating test results with defect tracking from CI/CD pipeline agents
not_for: Test execution (Qase manages cases, not execution)
Teams without structured test case management
Simple automated testing without traceability requirements
best_when: A modern engineering team wants AI agents to surface QA coverage gaps, analyze test result trends, and integrate test management data into CI/CD and issue tracking workflows.
avoid_when: Your test automation is code-only (pytest, Jest) without test case traceability needs.

auth.notes: Qase API token authentication. Tokens generated per user in profile settings. Workspace-scoped access.

pricing.notes: Modern test management with generous free tier. Competitive alternative to TestRail.

performance.rate_limits: Qase API: 5 req/sec on free; higher limits on paid plans

known_agent_gotchas: No public MCP server — REST API or SDK integration required
Free plan rate limit is 5 req/sec — burst carefully for large test suites
Qase has multiple reporter SDKs (pytest, jest) that auto-publish results — prefer these for CI
Test run status is async — poll for completion before reading results
Defect integration requires separate configuration per project
agent_readiness.security_notes: Test cases may document security-sensitive behavior. SOC2, GDPR. API token user-scoped. EU data residency available.

security_score_components.security_notes: Test case data may include security test documentation. SOC2, GDPR. EU data residency option.
